insight: When facing early Redeemer, if opponent gets it first, use a Wall to absorb 2/3 of its damage while staying ahead on economy, then buy your own Redeemer on the turn Gauss Cannon charges arrive.
author: p0lari
context: Discussion of defensive responses to opponent's early Redeemer in tricolor sets.

insight: Kinetic Driver as first attacker is generally better than Redeemer as first attacker; if you get Kinetic first then Redeemer next turn, you gain 4 out of 6 turns of attack value from Redeemer.
author: p0lari
context: Analysis of opening sequencing in tricolor sets with Kinetic Driver and Redeemer.

insight: Gauss Cannon and Iso Kronus are roughly equivalent in efficiency when all damage is relevant; choice between them depends on opponent's defense composition and how you'll spend remaining gold.
author: mtanzer
context: Discussion of unit efficiency in breach-proof strategies.

insight: Iso Kronus are worse as breach-proof economy because they can be killed on exhaust turn 1; as breach-proof offense against non-breach-proof, Gauss Cannon is preferred because Iso can be killed the turn before firing, resulting in one less total damage.
author: p0lari, mtanzer
context: Comparative analysis of Iso vs Gauss in breach-proof contexts.

insight: Units that prevent a set from being a standard 3rd Engineer opening include: natural Conduit access, Flame Animus into degenerate strategies, and Tatsu Nullifier (which is almost always a 3rd Engineer unit anyway).
author: crash_overlord
context: Discussion of which advanced units disrupt standard 3rd Engineer openings.

insight: Strong early red attackers (like Tantalum Ray or any good attacker) can punish 3rd Engineer play if the set lacks sufficient defensive tech; 3rd Engineer is vulnerable to aggressive openings.
author: p0lari
context: Analysis of when 3rd Engineer defensive strategies fail against good red attack.

insight: Thorium Dynamo can accelerate economy faster than standard Drone-Engineer pattern; P1 standard Dynamo opening is DD, D+Dynamo, DD; P2 is DD, Dynamo+Dynamo, D.
author: 307th
context: Explanation of optimal Thorium Dynamo opening sequences.

insight: Thorium Dynamo is slightly more efficient than a regular Drone as long as you don't waste any energy with it, whereas Auric Impulse functions as a drone you sell the next turn, costing you one energy (you get 4 gold next turn instead of 4 gold + 1 energy).
author: 307th, crash_overlord
context: Comparison of economy unit efficiency and mechanics.

insight: For breach-proof strategies, you typically need: a way to convert Drones into breach-proof economy (Thorium Dynamo, Trinity Drone, or Venge Cannon), a breach-proof attacker, and weak normal attack/defense in the set.
author: 307th, redzonex

insight: Two strong breach-proof combinations are: Thorium Dynamo + Iceblade, and Venge Cannon + Cluster Bolt (Venge Cannon converts Drones to attackers, freeing up Conduit green for Cluster Bolts).
author: 307th, redzonex
context: Identification of synergistic breach-proof unit combinations.

insight: Grimbotch has dramatically higher value than Tarsier in most practical situations; however, there are rare cases (e.g., sets with Defense Grid, Plexo Cell, Polywall, Infusion Grid) where Tarsier is better because Grimbotch won't get full value against heavy defensives.
author: 307th, crash_overlord
context: Discussion of when to choose long-term vs short-term attack units based on game state.

insight: Even a Grimbotch that only gets 4 swings (not full value) is approximately as good as a Tarsier; the key is that Grimbotch forces your opponent off making good investments through immediate damage, whereas long-term units require the game to extend.
author: mtanzer, crash_overlord
context: Analysis of short-term vs long-term unit value across game phases.

insight: At lower inflation, long-term attackers are relatively better than at higher inflation; this affects unit comparisons (e.g., a long-term and short-term unit equal at 1.45 inflation will not be equal at 1.5 or 1.4).
author: 307th, mtanzer
context: Mathematical discussion of how inflation affects unit value comparison.

insight: In breach-proof mirror endgames, if all tech supplies are exhausted, prefer Drones into Forcefields over Auric Impulse + Engineer chains; Drones provide 3 gold per 2 HP while paying for themselves.
author: crash_overlord, Deleted User
context: Discussion of resource allocation when supply limits are hit in breach-proof strategies.

insight: Short-term unit purchases (like Gaussite Symbiote pop, Nivo charges, Tia Threnody) are situation-dependent and particularly good post-absorb, under high opponent pressure, or when opponent soak is inefficient; they are bad early in low-pressure games.
author: 307th
context: Analysis of when short-term burst units should be prioritized.

insight: In base set + Proto hybrid sets, early absorption on Wall to enable Tarsier production is often better than rushing Proto, even when Proto can achieve 3 absorption.
author: 307th
context: Specific set analysis of absorption priority and tech choices.

insight: Galvantima (Galvani Drone + Antima Comet) is overrated; rushing Antima against Shredder is not a good strategy.
author: mrguy888
context: Brief assessment of Galvantima unit combination viability.
